what: Using Mahalanobis Distance for Anomaly Detection in Single-Cell RNA-Seq Data
title: Visualization of Cell Type Annotations
text: aim evaluate whether new batch query cells fits well within established reference cell types cells exhibiting unusual characteristics. use Mahalanobis distance calculated calculateDiscriminantSpace() function achieve . can extract analyze Mahalanobis distances query cells relative reference cell types. High Mahalanobis distances may indicate potential anomalies novel cell states. can now create visualizations illustrate distribution Mahalanobis distances highlight potential anomalies.  Low Mahalanobis Distances: query cells align well reference cell types, suggesting similar established cell types. High Mahalanobis Distances: Cells high distances may represent outliers novel, previously uncharacterized cell types. investigation may needed understand anomalies.
code: # Perform discriminant analysis and projection discriminant_results <- calculateDiscriminantSpace(   reference_data = reference_data,   query_data = query_data,   ref_cell_type_col = "expert_annotation",   query_cell_type_col = "SingleR_annotation",   calculate_metrics = TRUE,  # Compute Mahalanobis distance and cosine similarity   alpha = 0.01  # Significance level for Mahalanobis distance cutoff ) # Extract Mahalanobis distances mahalanobis_distances <- discriminant_results$query_mahalanobis_dist  # Identify anomalies based on a threshold threshold <- discriminant_results$mahalanobis_crit  # Use the computed cutoff value anomalies <- mahalanobis_distances[mahalanobis_distances > threshold] # Load ggplot2 for visualization library(ggplot2)  # Convert distances to a data frame distances_df <- data.frame(Distance = mahalanobis_distances,                            Anomaly = ifelse(mahalanobis_distances > threshold, "Anomaly", "Normal"))  # Plot histogram of Mahalanobis distances ggplot(distances_df, aes(x = Distance, fill = Anomaly)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5, position = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +   labs(title = "Histogram of Mahalanobis Distances",        x = "Mahalanobis Distance",        y = "Frequency") +   theme_bw()

title: Calculate Categorization Entropy — calculateCategorizationEntropy
text: function takes matrix category scores (cell type cells) calculates entropy category probabilities cell. gives sense confident cell type assignments . High entropy = lots plausible category assignments = low confidence. Low entropy = one two plausible categories = high confidence. confidence vernacular sense, "confidence interval" statistical sense. Also note entropy tells nothing whether assignments correct – see functionality package . functionality can used assessing comparatively confident different sets assignments (given number categories ).

title: Calculate Categorization Entropy — calculateCategorizationEntropy
text: function checks X already probability scale. Otherwise, applies softmax columnwise. can think entropies scale 0 maximum depends number categories. function entropy (minus input checking): entropy(p) = -sum(p*log(p)) . input vector p uniform distribution length(p) categories, entropy high possible.

title: Calculate Categorization Entropy — calculateCategorizationEntropy
text: Andrew Ghazi, andrew_ghazi@hms.harvard.edu

title: Calculate Categorization Entropy — calculateCategorizationEntropy
code: # Simulate 500 cells with scores on 4 possible cell types X <- rnorm(500 * 4) |> matrix(nrow = 4) X[1, 1:250] <- X[1, 1:250] + 5 # Make the first category highly scored in the first 250 cells  # The function will issue a message about softmaxing the scores, and the entropy histogram will be # bimodal since we made half of the cells clearly category 1 while the other half are roughly even. entropy_scores <- calculateCategorizationEntropy(X) #> X doesn't seem to be on the probability scale, applying column-wise softmax. #> Max possible entropy given 4 categories: 1.39

title: Calculate PCA Reconstruction Errors for Out-of-Distribution Anomaly Detection — calculateReconstructionError
text: PCA creates low-dimensional summary biological variation. computing local PCA space specifically reference cell type, algorithm learns strict biological rules governing specific cell state. query cell contains novel biological state (e.g., viral infection, unique drug response, actually unrepresented cell subtype hiding cluster), express genes local reference PCA ignores. query cell projected reference PCA space mathematically reconstructed, novel gene expressions lost. subtracting reconstructed matrix original matrix, function isolates "Residuals" (biology reference explain). Sum Squared Errors (SSE) residuals serves highly sensitive anomaly score novel biological states. function extracts reconstruction errors given object generates visualization. Four plot_type options available: "violin" (Default): Shows violin plot error distribution overlaid individual cell         points (jittered) colored anomaly status. violin trimmed data range maintain         statistical validity (preventing density estimation zero). "boxplot": Shows standard boxplot overlaid jittered points. "ridge": Shows ridgeline plots separating datasets vertically. vertical red dashed line         marks threshold. Best visualizing density non-anomalous cells versus long tail anomalies. "heatmap": Generates ComplexHeatmap showing Z-score scaled expression         highly variable genes used construct local PCA space. Cells grouped Dataset Anomaly Status.

title: Compare Marker Gene Expression between Query and Reference Data — compareMarkers
text: function performs following steps: 1. Optionally performs anomaly detection filters query cells based results. 2. Identifies marker genes cell type datasets using findMarkers approach. 3. Reference markers always computed using reference cells cell type. 4. Query markers computed using filtered cells (anomalous/non-anomalous) specified. 5. Compares overlap top marker genes corresponding cell types. 6. Evaluates expression consistency reference markers query data. 7. Provides quality scores based marker gene concordance. Marker genes identified using Wilcoxon rank-sum tests comparing cell type others. High overlap consistent expression markers indicate good annotation quality. S3 plot method creates scatter plot showing relationship marker overlap (x-axis) expression consistency (y-axis) cell type. Points colored quality score sized minimum number cells. Quality zones provide visual guidance interpretation.

Selecting highly variable genes (can be customized by the user) ref_top_genes <- getTopHVGs(ref_data_subset, n = 500) #> Warning: 'getTopHVGs' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::chooseHighlyVariableGenes' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'fitTrendVar' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::fitVarianceTrend' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'combineBlocks' is deprecated. #> See help("Deprecated") query_top_genes <- getTopHVGs(query_data_subset, n = 500) #> Warning: 'getTopHVGs' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::chooseHighlyVariableGenes' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'fitTrendVar' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::fitVarianceTrend' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'combineBlocks' is deprecated. #> See help("Deprecated")  # Intersect the gene symbols to obtain common genes common_genes <- intersect(ref_top_genes, query_top_genes) ref_data_subset <- ref_data_subset[common_genes,] query_data_subset <- query_data_subset[common_genes,]  # text: Anthony Christidis, anthony-alexander_christidis@hms.harvard.edu

title: Process PCA for SingleCellExperiment Objects — processPCA
code: library(SingleCellExperiment)  # Load data data("reference_data") data("query_data")  # Example 1: Dataset without PCA (will compute PCA) query_no_pca <- query_data reducedDims(query_no_pca) <- list()  # Remove existing PCA  processed_query <- processPCA(sce_object = query_no_pca, n_hvgs = 500) #> Data missing PCA - computing... #> Computing PCA... #> Warning: 'fitTrendVar' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::fitVarianceTrend' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'combineBlocks' is deprecated. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'scran::getTopHVGs' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::chooseHighlyVariableGenes' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Using 231 highly variable genes for PCA computation "PCA" %in% reducedDimNames(processed_query)  # Should be TRUE #> [1] TRUE ncol(processed_query)  # Should be 503 (unchanged) #> [1] 503  # Example 2: Dataset with existing valid PCA (will be preserved) processed_existing <- processPCA(sce_object = query_data, n_hvgs = 500) #> Data already has valid PCA - returning unchanged (503 cells) ncol(processed_existing)  # Should be 503 (unchanged, no downsampling) #> [1] 503  # Example 3: Large dataset requiring downsampling for PCA computation ref_no_pca <- reference_data reducedDims(ref_no_pca) <- list()  # Remove existing PCA  processed_large <- processPCA(sce_object = ref_no_pca,                               n_hvgs = 800,                               max_cells = 1000) #> Data missing PCA - computing... #> Downsampling data from 1500 to 1000 cells before PCA computation #> Computing PCA... #> Warning: 'fitTrendVar' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::fitVarianceTrend' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'combineBlocks' is deprecated. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'scran::getTopHVGs' is deprecated. #> Use 'scrapper::chooseHighlyVariableGenes' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Using 240 highly variable genes for PCA computation ncol(processed_large)  # Should be 1000 (downsampled for PCA computation) #> [1] 1000  # Example 4: Large dataset with existing PCA (no downsampling) processed_large_existing <- processPCA(sce_object = reference_data,                                        n_hvgs = 800,                                        max_cells = 1000) #> Data already has valid PCA - returning unchanged (1500 cells) ncol(processed_large_existing)  # Should be 1500 (preserved, no downsampling) #> [1] 1500

title: Fast Custom Linear Regression for Principal Components — regressFastCustom
text: function implements custom linear regression optimized categorical predictors commonly used single-cell RNA sequencing analysis. uses QR decomposition instead normal equations improved numerical stability handles rank-deficient design matrices gracefully. function supports various model specifications including: Simple cell type effects: PC ~ cell_type Cell type batch interactions: PC ~ cell_type * batch Cell type dataset interactions: PC ~ cell_type * dataset output format compatible speedglm results maintain consistency existing plotting analysis workflows.

title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: `scDiagnostics` comprehensive toolkit designed analysis diagnostics single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) data. package provides functionalities comparing principal components, visualizing canonical correlation analysis (CCA) outputs, plotting cell type-specific MDS PCA projections.

title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: package includes following key functionalities, organized specific purposes:

title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: Functions visualizing differences query reference datasets across multiple cell types. boxplotPCA: Boxplots PCA scores cell types. calculateDiscriminantSpace: Calculates discriminant space, plot method visualization. plotCellTypeMDS: Creates MDS plots cell types using query reference datasets. plotCellTypePCA: Plots principal components different cell types.

title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: Functions visualizing differences query reference datasets specific cell type. calculateGraphIntegration: Calculate graph network form communities cells. calculateWassersteinDistance: Wasserstein distance different cell types. comparePCA: Compares PCA results, plot method visualization. comparePCASubspace: Compares PCA subspace, plot method visualization. plotPairwiseDistancesDensity: Plots density pairwise distances.

what: Calculation of Statistical Measures to Compare Two Datasets
title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: Functions compute statistical measures compare two datasets. calculateAveragePairwiseCorrelation: Calculates average pairwise correlation, plot method visualization. calculateCramerPValue: Calculates p-value using Cramer's test. calculateHotellingPValue: Calculates p-value using Hotelling's T-squared test. calculateMMDPValue: Calculates p-value using maximum mean discrepancy. regressPC: Performs regression principal components, plot method visualization.

title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: Functions calculating overlap measures genes two datasets. calculateHVGOverlap: Calculates overlap highly variable genes (HVG) datasets. calculateVarImpOverlap: Calculates overlap variable importance measures datasets. compareMarkers: Compares marker genes datasets.

title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: Functions visualize compare expression markers reference query dataset. plotGeneExpressionDimred: Plots gene expression dimensionality reduction space. plotMarkerExpression: Plots marker expression levels.

title: scDiagnostics: Single-Cell Diagnostics Package — scDiagnostics-package
text: Functions detecting anomalies global cell type-specific levels. detectAnomaly: Detects anomalies data, plot method visualization. calculateCellSimilarityPCA: Calculates cell similarity PCA space, plot method visualization.
